 {"id":"bd-1aim.5","title":"Unit Tests: Transfer Module (No Mocks)","description":"Add comprehensive unit tests for the transfer module without mock infrastructure.

- rch/src/transfer.rs - **23 KB** - File transfer and rsync logic

   - Test compression (zstd) handling
   - Test incremental transfer
   - Test error handling (network failures)

2. **Path Handling Tests**
   - Test path normalization
   - Test symlink handling
   - Test special character escaping

3. **Artifact Retrieval Tests**
   - Test tar extraction
   - Test checksum verification
   - Test partial transfer recovery

## Constraints
- NO mock rsync
- Use real rsync to localhost for testing
- Test actual file transfers with temp directories
- Clean up transferred files after tests

-{"id":"bd-1ak0","title":"Unit Tests: State Management (No Mocks)","description":"Unit Tests: State Management (No Mocks)

Add comprehensive unit tests for state management without mock infrastructure.

## Current Status (Audit)
Existing coverage already present (from prior audit):
- primitives.rs: 17 tests
- lock.rs: 6 tests
- exit_codes.rs: 4 tests
- mod.rs: 3 tests

This bead now focuses on **logging compliance + edge-case expansion** to meet the no-mock standard.

## Target Files (rch/src/state/)
- mod.rs - Core state management module
- lock.rs - State locking mechanisms (15KB)
- exit_codes.rs - Exit code handling
- primitives.rs - State primitives and types (21KB)

## Remaining Test Requirements (No Mocks)
1. **Lock Tests** (lock.rs)
   - Add concurrent contention tests (real threads)
   - Add timeout/backoff coverage
   - Validate deadlock prevention in practice

2. **Exit Code Tests** (exit_codes.rs)
   - Add signal propagation edge cases
   - Validate mapping under error + panic conditions

3. **Primitives Tests** (primitives.rs)
   - Add serialization round-trip with real file I/O
   - Validate invalid inputs and defaults

4. **Integration Tests** (mod.rs)
   - Add state persistence round-trip on disk
   - Add concurrent state access with real locking

## Constraints
- NO MockFileSystem or fake persistence
- Use real temp directories
- Test actual file I/O
- Clean up temp files after tests

## Logging Requirements
- Each test: TEST START/TEST PASS format (per bd-2zsu)
- Log state transitions with timestamps
- Include file contents on persistence failures

## Acceptance Criteria
- Logging compliance across all state tests
- Edge cases covered beyond current 30 tests
- No flaky tests in concurrent scenarios","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T22:59:09.771839816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T23:37:16.021728468Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ak0","depends_on_id":"bd-1aim","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:37Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1bsa","title":"Performance benchmark UI overhead","description":"Create benchmarks in rch-common/benches/ui_bench.rs to measure rich_rust overhead:
- Measure baseline: plain text output time
- Measure rich output: formatted output time
- Calculate overhead percentage (target: <10%)
- Test high-frequency updates (progress bars)
- Memory allocation comparison

Benchmark scenarios:
1. Single line styled output (1000 iterations)
2. Table rendering (10 rows, 100 iterations)
3. Progress bar update (1000 updates)
4. Error panel rendering (100 iterations)
5. Full pipeline display (10 complete runs)

Technical requirements:
- Use criterion.rs for statistical benchmarking
- Generate flamegraph for profiling
- Compare release vs debug performance
- Test with different terminal widths
- Document baseline results in comments
- Set up CI to fail on >20% regression

Expected results:
- Single line: <100μs overhead
- Table (10 rows): <1ms overhead
- Progress update: <50μs overhead
- Full pipeline: <5ms overhead","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:11:34.465637827Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T21:14:00.277335114Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1bsa","depends_on_id":"bd-292h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1bsa","depends_on_id":"bd-fi8l","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:37Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1cwg","title":"Epic: True End-to-End Integration Tests (No Mocks)","description":"# Epic: True End-to-End Integration Tests (No Mocks)

## Executive Summary

This epic establishes a comprehensive suite of **100% realistic end-to-end integration tests** for RCH (Remote Compilation Helper). Unlike the existing mock-based test suite, these tests will:

- Connect to **real remote workers** via actual SSH
- Execute **real cargo/compiler commands** on those workers
- Transfer **real project files** via rsync
- Retrieve **real build artifacts** back to the local machine
- Validate **output correctness** against local execution

## Merged Scope: Fleet & Worker Lifecycle Tests (formerly bd-guef)

This epic now also includes the fleet and worker lifecycle E2E tests that were previously in a separate epic. This consolidation reduces duplication and provides a single canonical, comprehensive E2E suite.

### Fleet & Worker Lifecycle Coverage

1. **Multi-Worker Scenarios (bd-2s8m)**: Load balancing, priority selection, cached project locality, fleet scaling, heterogeneous workers
2. **Worker Lifecycle**: Registration, health monitoring, removal, updates, disconnects
3. **Test Logging Standardization (bd-2zsu)**: Structured logging across all test files

## Background & Motivation

### Current Test Gap Analysis

The existing RCH test suite provides good coverage for:
- Command classification logic (e2e_hook.rs)
- Daemon lifecycle and API (e2e_daemon.rs)
- Worker connectivity patterns (e2e_worker.rs)
- Fleet operations (e2e_fleet.rs)

However, all tests use **mock infrastructure**:
- `RCH_MOCK_SSH=1` environment variable bypasses real SSH
- `WorkersFixture::mock_local()` creates fake workers
- No actual compilation happens on remote machines
- No real artifact transfer is validated

### Why This Matters

RCH is a **transparent compilation offloading system** for AI coding agents. The agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor) have no awareness that their `cargo build` or `cargo test` commands execute remotely. This transparency requires **absolute correctness** in:

1. **Project synchronization** - Every file the build needs must arrive
2. **Command execution** - Exact same behavior as local execution
3. **Output capture** - Stdout/stderr must be byte-for-byte identical
4. **Exit codes** - Test failures (101), build errors (1) must propagate
5. **Artifact retrieval** - Compiled binaries must work locally

A bug in any of these areas breaks the illusion of local execution and causes agent confusion or failures.

## Test Categories

### Cargo/Rust Compilation (bd-12hi)
- cargo build, test, check, clippy, bench
- All CompilationKind variants

### SSH Infrastructure (bd-255k)
- Connection, authentication, command execution
- Output capture, timeout handling

### Project Sync (bd-1f2v)
- rsync transfer, excludes, incremental sync

### Artifact Transfer (bd-20zz)
- Binary retrieval, correctness verification

### Error Handling & Recovery (bd-23n3)
- Network failures, worker crashes, fail-open behavior
- Circuit breaker state transitions

### C/C++ Compilers (bd-v9pq)
- gcc, g++, clang, make, cmake, ninja

### Output Validation (bd-2ga8)
- Color preservation, ANSI codes, terminal semantics

### Performance & Timing (bd-1nhd)
- Hook classification <1ms, full pipeline <15% overhead

### Multi-Worker Scenarios (bd-2s8m)
- Load balancing, priority selection, heterogeneous workers
- Worker registration, health monitoring, lifecycle

### Test Logging Standardization (bd-2zsu)
- Structured JSON logging across all test files

## Success Criteria

- All CompilationKind variants tested with real workers
- Zero tolerance for output differences from local execution
- Exit codes must match exactly
- Tests pass reliably in CI with real infrastructure
- All tests produce structured, parseable logs
- Worker lifecycle scenarios pass in CI
- Multi-worker load balancing verified
- 100% structured logging compliance","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:10:09.472523589Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:27:10.595954929Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1cwg","depends_on_id":"bd-2s8m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1cwg","depends_on_id":"bd-2zsu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1cwg","depends_on_id":"bd-3saj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:37Z","created_by":"import"}]}

- Calculate and display percentiles (p50, p95, p99) for latency
- Reset counters option with --metrics-reset-interval

╰──────────────────────────────────────────────╯","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:07:56.185396572Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T04:10:59.877489588Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jru","depends_on_id":"bd-1z6p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3jru","depends_on_id":"bd-3u68","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3knb","title":"Add rich_rust dependency to workspace Cargo.toml","description":"# Add rich_rust dependency to workspace Cargo.toml

## Task Description

Add rich_rust as a workspace dependency in the root Cargo.toml, then add it to each relevant crate's Cargo.toml (rch, rchd, rch-common).

## Background

rich_rust is located at /dp/rich_rust during development. It provides:
- Console struct for terminal output
- Style and Color types for styling
- Table, Panel, Tree, ProgressBar renderables
- Markup parsing ([bold red]text[/])
- Automatic terminal capability detection

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Add to workspace Cargo.toml

```toml
[workspace.dependencies]
# During development, use path:
rich_rust = { path = "/dp/rich_rust", features = ["full"] }

# When published, switch to:
# rich_rust = { version = "0.1", features = ["full"] }
```

### 2. Add to rch/Cargo.toml

```toml
[dependencies]
rich_rust = { workspace = true, optional = true }

[features]
default = ["rich-ui"]
rich-ui = ["rich_rust"]
```

### 3. Add to rchd/Cargo.toml

Same as rch/Cargo.toml.

### 4. Add to rch-common/Cargo.toml

```toml
[dependencies]
# Minimal features for shared types
rich_rust = { workspace = true, optional = true, default-features = false }

[features]
default = ["rich-ui"]
rich-ui = ["rich_rust"]
```

## Technical Notes

### Why "full" features?
The full feature set includes:
- syntax: Syntax highlighting (syntect)
- markdown: Markdown rendering (pulldown-cmark)
- json: JSON pretty-printing (serde_json)

We want all of these for maximum capability.

### Why optional?
Making rich_rust optional allows:
1. Compile-time removal if issues arise
2. Smaller binaries for users who dont need rich output
3. Faster compilation without feature

### Path vs Published
During development, use path dependency for rapid iteration.
Switch to crates.io version when rich_rust is published.

## Verification

```bash
# Should compile successfully
cargo check --all-targets

# Should compile without rich-ui
cargo check --all-targets --no-default-features

# Should compile with rich-ui
cargo check --all-targets --features rich-ui
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. [ ] Workspace Cargo.toml has rich_rust in [workspace.dependencies]
2. [ ] rch/Cargo.toml has rich_rust with optional + feature flag
3. [ ] rchd/Cargo.toml has rich_rust with optional + feature flag
4. [ ] rch-common/Cargo.toml has rich_rust with minimal features
5. [ ] cargo check passes with default features
6. [ ] cargo check passes with --no-default-features","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:00:51.267049982Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T22:33:17.865319328Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T22:33:17.865271618Z","close_reason":"Added rich_rust as workspace dependency with optional feature flags in rch, rchd, and rch-common. Fixed unicode-width version conflict and clippy warnings in selection.rs.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3knb","depends_on_id":"bd-38kz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3n00","title":"Create Unit Tests for Test Harness & Utilities","description":"## Purpose
Create comprehensive unit tests for the test harness itself, ensuring the test infrastructure is reliable before using it to test rch.

## Why This Matters
- A buggy test harness produces false positives/negatives
- Harness bugs are harder to debug than rch bugs
- Unit tests catch harness issues before they affect e2e tests
- Provides documentation of expected harness behavior

## Test Categories

### 1. Logging Infrastructure Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/logging_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_log_entry_is_valid_json() {
    let logger = TestLogger::new("test_example");
    logger.info("test message", &[("key", "value")]);
    let log_content = std::fs::read_to_string(logger.log_path()).unwrap();
    for line in log_content.lines() {
        serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(line).expect("invalid JSON");
    }
}

#[test]
fn test_timing_accuracy() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_log_levels_filter_correctly() { ... }
```

### 2. Output Comparison Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/output_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_identical_outputs_compare_equal() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_path_normalization() {
    let local = "/home/user/project/src/main.rs:10:5";
    let remote = "/tmp/rch/project_abc/src/main.rs:10:5";
    let normalizer = OutputNormalizer::default();
    assert_eq!(normalizer.normalize(local), normalizer.normalize(remote));
}

#[test]
fn test_diff_shows_actual_differences() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_binary_comparison_exact() { ... }
```

### 3. Worker Discovery Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/discovery_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_workers_available_returns_false_when_no_config() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_skip_mechanism_logs_reason() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_worker_health_check_timeout() { ... }
```

### 4. Harness Lifecycle Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/harness_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_daemon_spawn_and_cleanup() {
    let harness = TrueE2EHarness::new_or_skip().unwrap();
    // Daemon should be running
    assert!(harness.daemon_is_healthy());
    drop(harness);
    // Daemon should be cleaned up (if harness spawned it)
}

#[test]
fn test_fixture_isolation() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_worker_project_cleanup() { ... }
```

### 5. Timing Utilities Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/timing_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_measure_command_timing_accuracy() {
    let start = Instant::now();
    let measured = measure_command_timing("sleep 0.1");
    let actual = start.elapsed();
    // Should be within 10ms of actual
    assert!((measured.as_millis() as i64 - actual.as_millis() as i64).abs() < 10);
}

#[test]
fn test_percentile_calculation() { ... }
```

## Test Organization
```
tests/
└── true_e2e/
    ├── mod.rs
    ├── harness.rs
    ├── logging.rs
    ├── output.rs
    └── tests/           # Unit tests for harness
        ├── mod.rs
        ├── logging_tests.rs
        ├── output_tests.rs
        ├── discovery_tests.rs
        ├── harness_tests.rs
        └── timing_tests.rs
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 100% code coverage for logging module
- [ ] All output comparison edge cases tested
- [ ] Worker discovery tests run without real workers
- [ ] Harness lifecycle tested end-to-end
- [ ] All tests pass with `cargo test` (no workers needed)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:32:54.582781639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:55:42.357344236Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T00:55:42.356337229Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3n00","depends_on_id":"bd-17tg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3n00","depends_on_id":"bd-1tq2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3n00","depends_on_id":"bd-3r2b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3n00","depends_on_id":"bd-3saj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:11:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3ndq","title":"Implement JobLifecycleLog for structured job event display","description":"Create JobLifecycleLog formatter in rchd/src/ui/jobs.rs for job event visualization:
- Job submission: job ID, source, command summary, assigned worker
- Job progress: compilation phase, elapsed time, resource usage
- Job completion: success/failure indicator, duration, artifact count
- Job failure: error type, worker state, suggested remediation

Technical requirements:
- Prefix each line with timestamp and job ID for log grep-ability
- Use consistent column alignment for scanability
- Color code by event type: blue (start), yellow (progress), green (success), red (failure)
- Include Unicode status icons inline
- Rate-limit progress updates (max 1/second) to prevent log spam
- Support both single-line compact and multi-line detailed modes

Format examples:
[14:30:01] [j-a3f2] ● START cargo build --release → worker1
[14:30:15] [j-a3f2] ◐ BUILD 14.0s │ Compiling foo v0.1.0 (45/120 crates)
[14:30:45] [j-a3f2] ✓ DONE  44.2s │ 156 artifacts │ cache written

 {"id":"bd-3o1l","title":"Idea: Dry-run offload (show pipeline steps)","description":"## Background
Users want to understand what RCH would do without actually offloading. A dry-run provides transparency and debugging.

## Goals
- Add `--dry-run` for hook/compile to show decision + estimated timings and transfer size.
- No network side effects in dry-run mode.

## Design / Approach
- Add dry-run flag to `rch diagnose` and `rch compile`.
- Run classification, selection simulation, and transfer size estimation without rsync/SSH exec.
- Emit JSON summary for automation.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Add dry-run flag plumbing in CLI and hook.
- Integrate estimator + selection explanation.
- Add docs/examples.

## Tests
- Unit: dry-run output schema.
- Integration: dry-run produces no SSH/rsync calls (mock asserts).
- E2E: dry-run output shown for a sample command.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dry-run provides a clear step-by-step summary.
- No side effects or network calls occur.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-3o1l.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Dry-run must not perform SSH/rsync side effects.
- Still runs classification and config validation.
- Output includes gating decisions + estimates.

## E2E Outline
- Dry-run produces JSON summary with steps.
- Mock counters confirm no network calls.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch/src/commands.rs: dry-run output schema.
- rch/src/hook.rs: dry-run path avoids side effects.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-3o1l.sh: dry-run produces step-by-step JSON summary; no SSH/rsync.

 {"id":"bd-3obh","title":"CRITICAL: Compile Command Hook Context Test","description":"## Test: rch compile in Hook Context

This test verifies that when an AI agent invokes `rch compile` (or any compilation command), the rich_rust integration does NOT interfere with the compilation output that the agent is parsing.

### Why This Is Critical

When Claude Code uses RCH, it:
1. Invokes `rch hook --stdin` with JSON describing the command
2. Expects JSON response with `allow`/`intercept` result
3. If intercepted, expects compilation stdout/stderr to pass through unchanged
4. Parses exit codes to determine success/failure

If rich output contaminates stdout, agents will fail to parse compilation results.

### Test Scenarios

#### Scenario 1: Hook Invocation (JSON Protocol)
```bash
# Test: Hook JSON response unchanged
INPUT='{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"cargo build"}}'
OUTPUT=$(echo "$INPUT" | rch hook --stdin)

# Verify: stdout is valid JSON
echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e . || fail "Not valid JSON"

# Verify: No ANSI codes in JSON
echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -qP '\x1b\[' && fail "ANSI codes in JSON!"
```

#### Scenario 2: Compilation Output Passthrough
```bash
# Test: cargo build output unchanged
cd /tmp/test_project
OUTPUT=$(rch compile cargo build 2>&1)

# Verify: rustc output preserved
echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "Compiling" || echo "WARN: No Compiling output"

# Verify: Exit code preserved
rch compile cargo build
EXIT=$?
[[ $EXIT -eq 0 ]] || [[ $EXIT -eq 101 ]] || fail "Unexpected exit: $EXIT"
```

#### Scenario 3: Error Output Preserved
```bash
# Create invalid Rust file
echo "fn main() { invalid syntax" > /tmp/test_project/src/main.rs

# Compile and capture error
OUTPUT=$(rch compile cargo build 2>&1)

# Verify: rustc error preserved
echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "error" || fail "Error not preserved"

# The error message itself should be parseable by agents
# Rich formatting should be on stderr, not mixed with compiler output
```

### Test Implementation

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/test_compile_hook_context.sh
set -euo pipefail

source scripts/test_lib.sh
init_test_log "compile_hook_context"

RCH="./target/release/rch"
TEST_PROJECT="/tmp/rch_test_$$"

setup() {
    log_json "setup" "Creating test project"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_PROJECT/src"
    cat > "$TEST_PROJECT/Cargo.toml" << 'EOF'
[package]
name = "test_project"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
EOF
    echo 'fn main() { println!("Hello"); }' > "$TEST_PROJECT/src/main.rs"
}

cleanup() {
    rm -rf "$TEST_PROJECT"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

setup

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 1: Hook invocation JSON purity
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "test" "Hook JSON purity test"

export RCH_HOOK_MODE=1
cd "$TEST_PROJECT"

HOOK_INPUT='{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"cargo build"}}'
STDOUT_FILE="$(mktemp)"
STDERR_FILE="$(mktemp)"

echo "$HOOK_INPUT" | "$RCH" hook --stdin > "$STDOUT_FILE" 2> "$STDERR_FILE"

# Check stdout
if ! jq -e . "$STDOUT_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    fail "stdout is not valid JSON"
fi

if grep -qP '\x1b\[' "$STDOUT_FILE"; then
    log_json "error" "ANSI codes found in stdout" "{\"content\":\"$(cat $STDOUT_FILE | head -c 500 | base64)\"}"
    fail "ANSI codes in hook stdout!"
fi

log_json "verify" "Hook stdout is clean JSON" '{"passed":true}'
unset RCH_HOOK_MODE

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 2: Compilation stdout/stderr separation
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "test" "Compilation output separation"

# Run compile with rich output potentially enabled
STDOUT_FILE="$(mktemp)"
STDERR_FILE="$(mktemp)"

"$RCH" compile cargo build --release > "$STDOUT_FILE" 2> "$STDERR_FILE" || true

# stdout should have compiler output (or be empty if intercepted)
# It should NOT have ANSI codes from RCH (compiler may add its own)
RCH_ANSI_PATTERN='\[rch\].*\x1b\['  # RCH-specific rich output pattern
if grep -qP "$RCH_ANSI_PATTERN" "$STDOUT_FILE"; then
    fail "RCH rich output leaked to stdout"
fi

log_json "verify" "stdout clean of RCH rich output" '{"passed":true}'

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 3: Exit code preservation
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "test" "Exit code preservation"

# Successful build
"$RCH" compile cargo build --release >/dev/null 2>&1
EXIT_SUCCESS=$?

# Failed build (introduce error)
echo 'fn main() { invalid' > "$TEST_PROJECT/src/main.rs"
"$RCH" compile cargo build --release >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
EXIT_FAIL=$?

# Restore
echo 'fn main() { println!("Hello"); }' > "$TEST_PROJECT/src/main.rs"

log_json "verify" "Exit codes" "{\"success\":$EXIT_SUCCESS,\"fail\":$EXIT_FAIL}"

if [[ $EXIT_SUCCESS -ne 0 ]] && [[ $EXIT_SUCCESS -ne 101 ]]; then
    log_json "warning" "Unexpected success exit code" "{\"exit\":$EXIT_SUCCESS}"
fi

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 4: Error message preservation
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "test" "Error message preservation"

echo 'fn main() { let x: i32 = "string"; }' > "$TEST_PROJECT/src/main.rs"
ERROR_OUTPUT="$(\"\\" compile cargo build 2>&1 || true)"

# Should contain rustc error (type mismatch)
if ! echo "$ERROR_OUTPUT" | grep -q "mismatched types\|expected.*found"; then
    log_json "warning" "Error message may be modified" "{\"output_sample\":\"$(echo $ERROR_OUTPUT | head -c 200)\"}"
fi

log_json "verify" "Error messages preserved" '{"passed":true}'

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SUMMARY
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "summary" "All compile hook context tests passed" '{"total_tests":4}'
echo ""
echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo "ALL COMPILE HOOK CONTEXT TESTS PASSED"
echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
```

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [ ] Hook JSON response contains no ANSI codes
2. [ ] Compilation stdout has no RCH rich output leakage
3. [ ] Exit codes preserved through rich integration
4. [ ] Compiler error messages preserved and parseable
5. [ ] Test runs in CI

### Files

+

@@ -0,0 +1,126 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+#
+# e2e_bd-3i0q.sh - Project-local .rchignore excludes
+#
+# Verifies:
+# - `rch diagnose` detects a project-local `.rchignore` file
+# - The reported exclude breakdown includes the expected `.rchignore` count
+# - Behavior is stable without `.rchignore`
+#
+# Notes:
+# - This script avoids cleanup commands (no rm -rf) by leaving temp dirs behind.
+#
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
+LOG_FILE="${PROJECT_ROOT}/target/e2e_bd-3i0q.jsonl"
+
+timestamp() {
+    date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ' 2>/dev/null || date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ'
+}
+
+log_json() {
+    local phase="$1"
+    local message="$2"
+    local extra="${3:-}"
+    if [[ -z "$extra" ]]; then
+        extra='{}'
+    fi
+    local ts
+    ts="$(timestamp)"
+    jq -nc \
+        --arg ts "$ts" \
+        --arg test "bd-3i0q" \
+        --arg phase "$phase" \
+        --arg message "$message" \
+        --argjson extra "$extra" \
+        '{ts:$ts,test:$test,phase:$phase,message:$message} + $extra' \
+        | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
+}
+
+die() {
+    log_json "error" "$*" '{"result":"fail"}'
+    exit 1
+}
+
+check_dependencies() {
+    log_json "setup" "Checking dependencies"
+    for cmd in cargo jq; do
+        command -v "$cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1 || die "Missing dependency: $cmd"
+    done
+}
+
+build_rch() {
+    local rch_bin="${PROJECT_ROOT}/target/debug/rch"
+    if [[ -x "$rch_bin" ]]; then
+        log_json "setup" "Using existing rch binary" "{\"path\":\"$rch_bin\"}"
+        echo "$rch_bin"
+        return
+    fi
+    log_json "setup" "Building rch (debug)"
+    (cd "$PROJECT_ROOT" && cargo build -p rch >/dev/null 2>&1) || die "cargo build failed"
+    [[ -x "$rch_bin" ]] || die "rch binary missing after build"
+    echo "$rch_bin"
+}
+
+test_no_rchignore() {
+    log_json "test" "Diagnose reports no .rchignore when absent"
+    local rch_bin
+    rch_bin="$(build_rch)"
+
+    local tmp_dir
+    tmp_dir="$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rchignore-none-XXXXXX")"
+
+    local output
+    output="$(cd "$tmp_dir" && "$rch_bin" diagnose "cargo build" 2>/dev/null)"
+
+    if echo "$output" | grep -qE "^\\s*\\.rchignore:" && echo "$output" | grep -q "not found"; then
+        log_json "verify" "No .rchignore detected as expected" '{"result":"pass"}'
+    else
+        die "Expected diagnose output to report .rchignore not found"
+    fi
+}
+
+test_rchignore_detected() {
+    log_json "test" "Diagnose detects .rchignore and reports pattern count"
+    local rch_bin
+    rch_bin="$(build_rch)"
+
+    local tmp_dir
+    tmp_dir="$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rchignore-present-XXXXXX")"
+
+    cat > "$tmp_dir/.rchignore" <<EOF
+large_data/
+secrets/
+EOF
+
+    local output
+    output="$(cd "$tmp_dir" && "$rch_bin" diagnose "cargo build" 2>/dev/null)"
+
+    if ! echo "$output" | grep -qE "^\\s*\\.rchignore:"; then
+        die "Expected diagnose output to include .rchignore section"
+    fi
+    if ! echo "$output" | grep -q "detected"; then
+        die "Expected diagnose output to report .rchignore detected"
+    fi
+    if ! echo "$output" | grep -qE ",\\s*2 from \\.rchignore\\)"; then
+        die "Expected exclude breakdown to include '2 from .rchignore'"
+    fi
+
+    log_json "verify" "Diagnose reports .rchignore detected with expected count" '{"result":"pass","rchignore_patterns":2}'
+}
+
+main() {
+    : > "$LOG_FILE"
+    check_dependencies
+
+    log_json "setup" "Starting bd-3i0q E2E tests"
+    test_no_rchignore
+    test_rchignore_detected
+    log_json "summary" "All bd-3i0q checks passed" '{"result":"pass","tests_run":2}'
+}
+
+main "$@"
+
